revision_priority: Fix the malformed bolding in the DiDi project bullet so the quantifier reads cleanly and ATS-safe.
Add one explicit database/storage-performance sentence or bullet for the DocumentDB fit, rather than relying on generic backend wording.
revision_instructions: Keep the resume structure unchanged, but make two targeted edits. First, in the DiDi project bullet ending with the internal-user metric, replace the broken emphasis `140+ week**ly** internal users` with a clean ATS-safe version such as `**140+** weekly internal users`. Second, in the TikTok or Summary section, add one sentence or bullet that connects the Java/C++ work to database-facing concerns relevant to DocumentDB, such as query latency, retrieval correctness, persistence validation, schema handling, or storage-path debugging. Do not remove any required JD technologies; the goal is to make the database fit more explicit, not to reduce scope.
